description_long: This object is a set of five axes, each featuring a robust, dark-colored metal head and a straight, light wooden handle. The axe heads are rectangular with a flat poll and a single sharp cutting edge, designed for chopping or splitting tasks. The handles are cylindrical and appear to be made of smooth, unfinished wood, providing a comfortable grip for manual use. The metal heads are securely mounted onto the handles, with no visible fasteners or decorative elements. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and function rather than ornamentation. These axes are typical of those found in urban garages, maintenance sheds, or used by municipal workers for landscaping and emergency tasks.
description: A set of five utilitarian axes with dark metal heads and straight, light wooden handles, designed for chopping or splitting tasks. The design is simple, robust, and functional, suitable for urban maintenance or emergency use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat faces of the axe heads and the straight alignment of the wooden handles.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin, sharp cutting edges of the axe heads and the full length of the handles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat polls of the axe heads and the handles extending backward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the blade thickness and the straight profile of the handles.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, smooth finish, no visible grip features, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Rectangular single-edged blade with a flat poll, no beard, and a straight cutting edge.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle.
